metadata.title:
SAY ROYAL ARCH


PLANS A BOYCOTT

pages.raw_text:
With local option in a majority
of the counties of Oregon. both
parties either pledged to local OPP
ton or prohibition in Washington,
local option made a feature of the
republican platform in Idaho, and
many counties Of California crying
for that law, the saloon men of
the Pacific coast states face a criti-
cal situation. And now comes Eu-
gene w. Chain, the prohibition
candidate for president, on tour
through the west and coast states,
speaking to crowds that equal the
biggest turnouts for either of the
old parties with their best speakers
as attractions.


The saloon men are anxious.
That is evident. Their action in this
city shows they have prepared for
war an dare determined to retain
their saloons at any cost. The sa-
loon men of the coast states are
organized in a society known as
the Knights OF the Royal Arch.
Saloon owners, brewery owners,
managers, bartenders, drivers, beer
makers. in fact every one connect
ed with the liquor business in any
way. Is eligible to membership.
